zh.lifeSummary: 當Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais於1311年1月出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德時，她的父親Jean de CLERMONT29歲，母親Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu26歲。她於1327年5月與Jean d'Armagnac I在法國格斯阿馬尼亞克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1365年8月25日在法國奧其坦尼格斯奧享年54歲，埋葬在法國奧其坦尼艾維倫羅德茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais于1311年1月出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德时，她的父亲Jean de CLERMONT29岁，母亲Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu26岁。她于1327年5月与Jean d'Armagnac I在法国格斯阿馬尼亞克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1365年8月25日在法国奧其坦尼格斯奧享年54岁，埋葬在法国奧其坦尼艾維倫羅德茲。

nameMeanings.name: Clermont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Georges, Normand, Pierre, Armand, Edouard, Gaston, Ghislaine, Gilles, Henri, Herve, Jean Francois.

French and English (Middlesex, of Norman and French Huguenot origin): habitational name from any of various places in France called (Le) Clermont, from Old French clair, cler ‘bright, clear’ + mont ‘hill’, i.e. a hill that could be seen a long way off. Compare Clairmont .

English (Middlesex, of Norman and possibly also Huguenot origin): variant of Claremont, from the Old French female personal name Claremonde, supposedly from ancient Germanic Claremunda, a hybrid compound of Latin clarus ‘famous’ + ancient Germanic mund ‘protection’.

variants.name: Clairmont
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adelard, Aime, Antoine, Aurore, Fernand.
French: variant of Clermont and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Clairmont is very rare in France.
In some cases possibly also English: variant of Claremont or Clermont . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Béatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais was born in January 1311, in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, her father, Jean de CLERMONT, was 29 and her mother, Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, was 26. She married Jean d'Armagnac I in May 1327, in Armagnac, Gers, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 25 August 1365, in Auch, Gers, Occitanie, France, at the age of 54, and was buried in Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais est née en janvier 1311, à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Jean de CLERMONT, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Jean d'Armagnac I en mai 1327, à Armagnac, Gers, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 août 1365, à Auch, Gers, Occitanie, France, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais im Januar 1311 in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jean de CLERMONT 29 und ihre Mutter Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu 26. Sie hat im Mai 1327 in Armagnac, Gers, Frankreich Jean d'Armagnac I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. August 1365 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Auch, Gers, Occitanie, Frankreich und wurde in Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais è nata il nel gennaio 1311 nel Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, sua padre, Jean de CLERMONT, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Jean d'Armagnac I nel maggio 1327, nel Armagnac, Gers, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 25 agosto 1365, nel Auch, Gers, Occitanie, Francia, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolaisが1311年1月にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドで生まれた時，父Jean de CLERMONTは29歳，母Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheuは26歳であった。1327年5月にフランスジャーズアルマグナックでJean d'Armagnac Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1365年8月25日にフランスオクシタニージャーズアウチで54歳で死去し，フランスオクシタニーエイビーロンロデズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais(이)가 1311년 1월 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jean de CLERMONT(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1327년 5월 프랑스 그르스 아마그나크에서 Jean d'Armagnac I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 8월 25일 프랑스 옥시탠이 그르스 오치에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 옥시탠이 에비런 로데즈에 묻혔다.

pt.Mai 1327: de 9 de maio de 1327 a 8 de junho de 1327
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais nasceu em janeiro de 1311, em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Jean de CLERMONT, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean d'Armagnac I em maio de 1327, em Armagnac, Gers, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de agosto de 1365, em Auch, Gers, Occitanie, França, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais родилась январь 1311 г. в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Jean de CLERMONT было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean d'Armagnac I май 1327 г. в местности армагнак, джирс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 25 августа 1365 г. в местности ауч, джирс, окситани, Франция в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности родез, авирон, окситани, Франция.

es.Mai 1327: desde 9 de mayo de 1327 hasta 8 de junio de 1327
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Béatrice De Clermont Countess Of Charolais nació en enero de 1311, en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Jean de CLERMONT, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Jeanne d'Argies Dame de Catheu, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Jean d'Armagnac I en mayo de 1327, en Armagnac, Gers, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 25 de agosto de 1365, en Auch, Gers, Occitanie, Francia, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Rodez, Aveyron, Occitanie, Francia.
